info.title: FB Group F&B Platform API
info.description: Singapore F&B CRM / QR ordering / loyalty platform (PoC).

post.description: Grant or WITHDRAW marketing consent (PDPA withdrawal right) — records an audit event.

get.description:
The orderable leaf stalls in a node's scope — a chain shows its whole group's stalls, a
storefront shows itself. Powers the 'QR Menu' for a Chain row (a group/brand browse).

Full menu for a stall reachable in the node's scope — validated so a node link can only
reach stalls actually within that node (its leaves or stalls leased into it).

Full menu for one stall reachable at the token's venue — the venue's own stalls OR stalls
leased in from another owner. A menu that is neither at the venue nor leased in is rejected,
so a QR code can never reach an arbitrary outlet's menu.

schema.title: Response List Orders Api V1 Orders Get

get.description: Printable receipt payload — company header (console) + outlet/stall + lines + payment.

schema.title: Response List Customers Api V1 Crm Customers Get
Response Segment Summary Api V1 Crm Segments Get
Response Customer Timeline Api V1 Crm Customers  Customer Id  Timeline Get
Response List Customer Tasks Api V1 Crm Customers  Customer Id  Tasks Get
Response My Open Tasks Api V1 Crm Tasks Get
Response List Opportunities Api V1 Crm Opportunities Get
Response Customer Opportunities Api V1 Crm Customers  Customer Id  Opportunities Get
Response List Activities Api V1 Crm Customers  Customer Id  Activities Get

get.description:
Per-CHILD breakdown for the member-tree drill-down: Platform → each tenant; a node → its
direct children (each child's whole-subtree totals). Same RBAC as the other reports.

AI growth advisor — executive summary + ranked next-best actions. Uses Claude when
configured, else a deterministic heuristic. Scoped like the other reports.

schema.title: Response My Orders Api V1 Me Orders Get
Response My Vouchers Api V1 Me Vouchers Get
Response Reward Catalog Api V1 Me Rewards Catalog Get

get.description:
The operator's platform capabilities — drives which console sections/actions render.
Any platform-tier user may read their own; non-operators get an empty set.

schema.title: Response Merchants Api V1 Platform Merchants Get
Response Coalitions Api V1 Platform Coalitions Get
Response List Operators Api V1 Platform Operators Get
Response List Campaigns Api V1 Campaigns Get

post.description: Issue the campaign's configured voucher to its audience (granted-voucher issuer).

schema.title: Response List Promotions Api V1 Promotions Get

get.description:
Outlets (+ their active menu id) for the merchant. When `node_id` is given the list is scoped
to that member-tree node's subtree — its sellable storefronts only (menu.id == node id) — so
'Enter'-ing any chain shows just its own outlets. Always intersected with the caller's RBAC.

schema.title: Response List Users Api V1 Admin Users Get

get.description:
Caller's effective permissions in a merchant context — the capabilities contract for
client nav-gating. Server still enforces per-route; this only prunes the menu.

The caller's visible slice of the member-tree (flat; client assembles via parent_id).
Each node carries `can_manage` (may grow the tree here) + `qr_path` (a customer-scan link).

post.description: Create a child node under a parent the caller manages — any depth (enterprise→stall).

patch.description: Rename / (de)activate a node the caller manages.

schema.title: Response List Node Accounts Api V1 Org Nodes  Node Id  Accounts Get

post.description: Set/reset a staff member's POS PIN (manager of the node; unique within the merchant).

schema.title: Response List Pos Staff Api V1 Org Nodes  Node Id  Pos Staff Get

post.description: Add a till operator; returns the one-time PIN (show-once — PINs are hashed at rest).
Mint a fresh storefront-unique PIN; returns it once.

schema.title: Response List Venue Leases Api V1 Org Nodes  Node Id  Leases Get
Response List Brands Api V1 Org Brands Get
Response List Outlets Api V1 Org Outlets Get
Response List Tables Api V1 Org Outlets  Outlet Id  Tables Get

get.description:
Staff: an attached diner's UNUSED vouchers at this merchant — so the cashier can apply one
without the diner's phone.

schema.title: Response Diner Vouchers Api V1 Vouchers Diner  Customer Id  Get

get.description: Dry-run: validate a scanned/typed voucher (no mutation) so the cashier can confirm before applying.

post.description: Cashier redeems a voucher (scan QR / enter code) → marks it used + applies its value to the order.

ConsentUpdateRequest.title: ConsentUpdateRequest
ConsentUpdateRequest.description: Grant or withdraw marketing consent (the PDPA withdrawal path), from the customer profile.

LeaseOut.title: LeaseOut
LeaseOut.description:
One tenancy at a venue. `rent_type` is the foodcourt/coffeeshop switch: FIXED = flat $/mo
(landlord blind); GTO = % of turnover (landlord reads turnover). `rate` is $/mo for FIXED, a
percentage for GTO.

MerchantOrderOut.title: MerchantOrderOut
MerchantOrderOut.description: Row in the merchant-wide orders feed — order + items + resolved labels.

MerchantUpdateIn.title: MerchantUpdateIn
MerchantUpdateIn.description: Operator-side merchant edits: rename and/or flip module flags. All optional.

MyPermissionsOut.title: MyPermissionsOut
MyPermissionsOut.description:
The caller's effective permission codes in a merchant context — the capabilities
contract that drives client-side nav rendering. `permissions` is expanded (never the
raw '*' wildcard); `is_super_admin` short-circuits to "can do everything".

NavFlagsOut.title: NavFlagsOut
NavFlagsOut.description:
Minimal, non-sensitive UI toggles any staff member may read to render nav —
deliberately omits economic config (spin costs, earn rates). Full settings stay
owner-only (`merchant.manage`); these "which features the venue runs" booleans are
already public (the anonymous QR context exposes rewards/ordering to diners).

`can_manage_merchant` is the caller's capability (holds `merchant.manage` → owner or
operator), so the client can hide owner-only nav (Settings / Team) without a second call.

NodeBrowseOut.title: NodeBrowseOut
NodeBrowseOut.description:
A node-scoped customer browse (the 'brand / group app' view): point at any member-tree node
and see the orderable leaf stalls in its scope (its own sellable leaves + any stalls leased into
a venue within it). `is_group` = it's a chain (many stalls) vs a single storefront.

OrgNodeCreateOut.title: OrgNodeCreateOut
OrgNodeCreateOut.description:
A freshly-created node + (for a Storefront) its auto-provisioned starter POS team with one-time
PINs for the owner to hand out. `pos_team` is empty for a Chain or if a team already existed.

OrgNodeOut.title: OrgNodeOut
OrgNodeOut.description:
One node of the member-tree, flat (the client assembles the tree via `parent_id`).
`role` is CHAIN or STOREFRONT; `sells` is the engine truth. `can_manage` = whether THIS
caller may create/rename beneath it (downline-only).

OrgTreeOut.title: OrgTreeOut
OrgTreeOut.description: The caller's visible slice of the member-tree + whether they can grow it at all.

PinLoginRequest.title: PinLoginRequest
PinLoginRequest.description:
POS quick-login — resolves the till operator at the bound storefront (`outlet_id`) whose PIN
matches. PINs are unique per storefront; `merchant_id` carries the suspend/availability check.

PlatformCapabilitiesOut.title: PlatformCapabilitiesOut
PlatformCapabilitiesOut.description: The operator's platform-tier capabilities — drives operator-console section/action gating.

PosStaffOut.title: PosStaffOut
PosStaffOut.description: A till operator at a storefront. No email/password is surfaced — POS users are PIN-only.

PosStaffSecret.title: PosStaffSecret
PosStaffSecret.description: Show-once payload: the plaintext PIN is returned exactly once (PINs are bcrypt-hashed at rest).

ReceiptCfg.title: ReceiptCfg
ReceiptCfg.description: Company header printed on POS receipts (configured at the console).

VoucherConfigIn.title: VoucherConfigIn
VoucherConfigIn.description: Vouchers a campaign issues to its audience (the granted-voucher issuer).

WelcomeVoucherCfg.title: WelcomeVoucherCfg
WelcomeVoucherCfg.description:
Welcome voucher pack granted on signup (the "10× $1, one per day" campaign). Stored in
merchants.settings; issued by services/vouchers.issue_welcome_pack on registration.
